summary: abi-encode@1.0.2 ships base64-decoded payload execution in main entry
details: The package's index.js contains multiple Buffer.from(..., "base64") decode operations combined with.toString() conversions at lines 9, 27, 49, and 67. The package name suggests ABI encoding utilities, but the main module's structure — hidden base64-encoded blobs decoded and stringified at module top level — is inconsistent with a legitimate encoding library, which would implement encoding algorithms in plain source. The pattern matches obfuscated-payload delivery: encoded bytes shipped in source, decoded on require, and evaluated or executed. Traced-code review of this file was withheld by the provider's malware-output safety filter, which typically fires on content matching operational credential-theft or exfiltration code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
